mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production index (INDPRO) signal expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If rolling 1-year correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns is non-positive over any 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it indicates persistent labor market slack and weak consumer demand, which depresses corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical inverse relationship between unemployment and equities
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE>8% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects broad-based demand strength across both residential investment and consumption, generating positive short-term momentum in equity markets as confidence improves.
failure_condition: If a composite of HOUST and RSAFS normalized changes fails to show a positive correlation with 5-day forward returns (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%), further conventional easing is impossible, but the commitment to keep rates low supports equity valuations through lower discount rates and search for yield, leading to positive returns over 20-day horizons.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are not consistently positive or are lower than in non-ZLB periods during ZLB episodes in walk-forward data.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production index rises above 95, it signals the manufacturing sector is operating at above normal capacity, indicating robust supply activity that boosts earnings and drives equity prices higher over the next week.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following INDPRO>95 trigger are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p<0.05) over walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of monthly PAYEMS changes with 20-day forward returns is ≤ 0 over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rate combined with low consumer price inflation results in negative real interest rates, which incentivizes borrowing and risk-taking, pushing equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon. The interaction is crucial because the effect depends on the joint state of the rate and inflation.
failure_condition: If the ratio of FEDFUNDS to CPIAUCSL change (or real rates proxy) does not have a significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (p<0.05) in walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: During the housing market recovery phase following a bust, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal a durable turnaround in residential investment that strongly boosts equity markets as it reduces downside risks. This effect is particularly strong in the early recovery regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing recovery leads equities in early cycle
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST changes and 5-day forward returns is not positive during periods classified as early housing recovery (HOUST<600 and rising) in walk-forward.
